finding: Theme type mismatch: `ResolvedTheme = Record<ThemeToken, ColorValue>` (types.ts:70) is color-only. Animation tokens are durations and cubic-bezier values — they cannot fit in `ColorValue` (which is `HexColor | CssVarRef`). The plan does not address this type safety gap. Requires either: (a) a separate `AnimationTokens` type and registry, or (b) widening `ResolvedTheme` with a union type for non-color values. Both approaches need explicit design.
repair_path: Define a `DurationValue` and `EasingValue` type separate from `ColorValue`. Create a `ResolvedAnimationTokens` record type, or change `ResolvedTheme` to include a `durations` and `easing` sub-object. Update `resolveThemeVariant` to return both color and animation tokens. Do not try to fit durations into a `ColorValue` slot.

finding: Surface area creep: The plan bundles 5 independent work items — (1) animation design tokens, (2) Slider component, (3) ColorPicker component, (4) EmptyState component, (5) v1 migration cleanup — across 14+ files. These have no runtime dependency on each other. Any one of these items contains enough work for a lane. Bundling them makes review, testing, and revert impossible to decouple. Specifically: animation tokens modify public types (types.ts, resolve.ts), new components create 9 new files, v1 cleanup is exploratory with indeterminate scope.
repair_path: Split into at minimum 2 lanes: (A) Animation token system — CSS variables + type changes + token resolution only, without modifying existing component CSS. (B) New components — Slider, ColorPicker, EmptyState as a batch since they're all additive. Remove v1 cleanup (Work Item 5) entirely from this plan — it's exploratory research, not an execution plan. It can become a separate cartographer/explore task.

finding: Error clarity gap: Zero consideration for error states across all 3 new components. Slider: what happens when `min > max`, `step ≤ 0`, or `value` is out of range? Does it clamp, throw, or warn? ColorPicker: invalid hex input (`#GGG`, `#FF` partial) has no specified validation behavior — silent fallback or user-visible error? EmptyState: low surface, but icon slot failure (missing/errored icon) not mentioned. Animation tokens: no CSS `@property` registration for type-checked custom properties.
repair_path: For each component: (a) define error states with `aria-invalid`, (b) add console.warn for invalid Slider props (min > max), (c) specify ColorPicker validation behavior (on input vs on blur, fallback for invalid hex), (d) add `@property` rules in theme.css for animation duration/easing types so invalid values don't silently cascade.

finding: CSS naming inconsistency: Existing v2 CSS variables use the `--v2-*` prefix consistently (`--v2-background-bg-base`, `--v2-text-text-base`). The proposed animation tokens (`--duration-fast: 85ms`, `--ease-expressive-out: ...`) use unprefixed names. This creates a namespace mismatch in the theme system. Developers looking at `--v2-background-bg-base` and `--duration-fast` in the same codebase will not know which namespace they belong to, and future tooling (CSS variable autocomplete, theme extraction) will need special-casing.
repair_path: Prefix animation tokens with `--v2-*` to match existing convention: `--v2-duration-fast`, `--v2-duration-normal`, `--v2-ease-expressive-out`, etc. This keeps the entire v2 design token namespace uniform and makes migration to a future token extraction tool consistent.

finding: Coupling animation tokens to existing components: The plan says "Apply to existing components: Add transitions to button-v2.css and dialog-v2.css" as part of Work Item 1. This creates coupling between the animation token system and specific component implementations. If the tokens are still being finalized (naming, values), then modifying existing components in the same change makes revert/revision more costly. The transition values in existing components should be updated in a separate follow-up change after tokens are stable.
repair_path: Split into: (1a) Animation token system — only CSS variable declarations + type changes + resolve.ts registration. (1b) Token adoption — a separate change that updates button-v2.css, dialog-v2.css, and all other component CSS files to use the new animation CSS variables instead of hardcoded values. This makes 1a additive/reversible and keeps 1b as pure find-and-replace.

finding: Inconsistent data-component convention: The plan specifies `data-component="slider-v2"` for Slider but does not mention this convention for ColorPicker or EmptyState. All existing v2 components use the `[data-component="xxx-v2"]` CSS selector pattern (checkbox-v2, tooltip-v2, accordion-v2, etc.). Missing this attribute on the new components breaks the CSS convention and makes them inconsistent for any future tooling that discovers components by data attribute.
repair_path: Add `data-component="color-picker-v2"` and `data-component="empty-state-v2"` to the specification for ColorPicker and EmptyState. The plan should explicitly state that ALL new v2 components follow this convention.

finding: Missing testing strategy: The plan requires Storybook stories for all 3 components (good for visual regression) but does not mention unit tests, accessibility tests, or integration tests. Slider (Kobalte-based, complex a11y: keyboard navigation, ARIA slider role, value announcements) and ColorPicker (hex validation, localStorage persistence for recent colors) in particular need test coverage beyond storybook rendering.
repair_path: Add test files for each component: `slider-v2.test.tsx` (min/max clamping, keyboard nav, orientation), `color-picker-v2.test.tsx` (hex input validation, recent colors persistence fallback), `empty-state-v2.test.tsx` (variant rendering, icon fallback). Use `@solidjs/testing-library`. Add aria-* attribute assertions.

finding: Addressed critic's type mismatch concern: Animation tokens will be added directly to v2/styles/theme.css as --v2-duration-* and --v2-ease-* CSS variables, NOT widened in ResolvedTheme. The ResolvedTheme type is for per-theme color values; animation tokens are static CSS primitives. This follows the established v2 CSS variable naming convention and avoids breaking existing theme consumers.
repair_path: Add animation CSS vars to v2/styles/theme.css, add documentation in theme/types.ts as a comment, add static animation tokens to animations.css
